title: Rhythm: full markup paragraph
description: Rhythm exercise with HTML + code + prose mixed

tips.text: Nothing new to learn here. We hold the rhythm on a handful of ordinary words before the markup mixes in.
Keep your fingers on home position and your eyes ahead. Repeat each word at one steady pace, the same beat from first key to last. Don't rush.

tips.title: Keep it even
tips.text: Listen for a regular click and keep to it, like a clock in a quiet room. Slow and even beats fast and ragged. Even keystrokes read like clean copy.

tips.title: Rhythm: welcome
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: welcome welcome welcome. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: open
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: open open open. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: press
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: press press press. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.text: Tags and prose share the same keyboard and the same need for a steady hand. Set the rhythm right on plain words, and the mixed lines will follow it.
Worked through every step? Open the trainer below and make one full run on the lesson text. That run saves your progress and unlocks the next lesson.

character_tips.anna: Web copy + tags + JSON - a typical webpage source.
character_tips.maxim: Modern coding = mixed media: markup, prose, data. All in one paragraph.
character_tips.knopych: MEGA MIX! Tags + words + data! Web flow! 🌊
character_tips.klavochka: All the symbols dancing together! What a show! 🎭
